english: being in pieces, in fragments C., cf. bun-loṅ (bun long).

1. pf. and imp. of lón-ba (lon pa). - 2. in conjunction with spyód-pa (spyod pa): to use, to make use of, to have the use or benefit of, to enjoy, e.g. bdé-ba (bde ba) daṅ (dang) skyíd-pa (skyid pa) - la happiness and prosperity; lóṅ-spyod-par (long spyod par) byá-bai (bya ba'i) rgyu (rgyu) the object of enjoyment, the thing enjoyed Stg.; loṅs-spyód (longs spyod) (Ssk. भोग) 1. enjoyment, fruition, use, esp. with regard to eating and drinking, loṅs-spyód (longs spyod) śá-la (sha la) byed (byed) they fed on meat, loṅs-spyód (longs spyod) śiṅ (shing) dé-las (de las) byed (byed) they lived on (the fruits of) this tree Pth. 2. plenty, abundance, bza-btuṅ-gi (bza' btung gi) loṅs-spyod (longs spyod) dpag-tu-méd-pa (dpag tu med pa) bsag (bsag) Glr. they produced or procured an enormous quantity of food and drink; esp.: riches, loṅs-spyod (longs spyod) čé-ba (che ba) great riches; wealth, property, lóns-spyod-kyi (longs spyod kyi) bdág-por (bdag por) gyur (gyur) he became owner of the property Dzl.; mčód-pa (mchod pa) byá-bai (bya ba'i) lóṅs-spyod (longs spyod) med (med) he was not rich enough to bring an offering (to Buddha) Dzl.
Sch.: 'half through, through the middle, one half (?)'.

notice, tidings, message, lon-bzaṅ (lon bzang) good news, spriṅ-ba (spring ba) to give notice, send word, send a message; lon (lon) kyur (kyur) or lon (lon) zer (zer) has also the special sense: send in my name! C.; lon (lon) žig (zhig) ḵyér-la (khyer la) śoṅ (shong) let me know, send me word Pth

= lén-pa (len pa) 1. to take, to receive etc. Glr., Pth., ču (chu) lón-nam (lon nam) have you fetched the water? i.e. are you bringing the water? Pth.; nór-bu (nor bu) mi (mi) lon (lon) I shall not receive the jewel! Pth. - 2. more frq. the word is used with reference to time: to elapse, to pass, a. in a general sense, lo (lo) máṅ-po (mang po) žig (zhig) lón-pa (lon pa) daṅ (dang) after many years had elapsed Dzl., riṅ-žig (ring zhig) lón-te (lon te) after a long time, riṅ-por (ring por) ma (ma) lón-par (lon par) after a short time. b. with regard to the age of a person: lo (lo) ći (ci) tsam (tsam) lon (lon) how old are you? bču-drúg-lon (bcu drug lon) I am sixteen Mil.
W. sometimes for lo (lo) year, and lób-ma (lob ma) for ló-ma (lo ma) leaf.
pf., imp. lobs (lobs), to learn, rarely for slób-pa (slob pa); lobs-pa (lobs pa) the act of learning Dzl.
in truth, indeed, mgón-skyabs (mgon skyabs) raṅ (rang) los (los) yin (yin) he is indeed the helper (from a hymn in praise of Buddha).
1. the letter śa (sha), the English sh, but palatal; in C. it is distinguished from ཞ (ža) (zha) only by the following vowel being sounded in the high tone. - 2. num.: 27.

I. 1. flesh, meat, gyág-śa (g.yag sha) yak's flesh, lúg-śa (lug sha) mutton; śa (sha) °tsód-pa (°tsod pa) (W. *tsó-če* (*tso che*)) to boil meat; śa (sha) rṅod-pa (rngod pa) (W. *°ṅó-če* (*°ngo che*), *śrág-čè* (*shrag che*) or *lám-čè* (*lam che*)) to roast meat; 'pyí-śa ('phyi sha) outward flesh, náṅ-śa (nang sha) or náṅ-ča (nang cha) inward flesh, or the entrails Cs.(?); śá-nas (sha nas) čád-pai (chad pa'i) bu (bu) Glr. the child of my own flesh and blood; śa (sha) krig-pa (krig pa) sexual instinct; *'á-pe śa* (*'a pe sha*); *'á-me śa* (*'a me sha*) in W. a vulgar form of attestation; surface of the body, śai (sha'i) ri-mo (ri mo) spots, stripes etc. on the skin (of an animal) Tar. - 2. muscle, nú-śa (nu sha) thoracic muscle Mṅg. - 2. for śa-kóg (sha kog) v. compounds. II. v. śá-ba (sha ba) and śa-mo (sha mo). Comp. śa-bkra (sha bkra) n. of a cutaneous disease Med. - śa-skám (sha skam) meat dried in the sun. - śa-ḵáṅ (sha khang) larder; butcher's stall. - śa-ḵú (sha khu) broth. - śa-kóg (sha kog) the body of a slaughtered animal,
